zh.homeIntro1: 宾根的希尔德加德（Hildegard von Bingen，1098年—1179年9月17日），是中世纪德国本笃会女修院院长、神秘主义者、作曲家、博物学家和医学家。她自幼即有神视体验，声称能看到「活的光」（Lux vivens），并将这些神视记录在多部著作中。
zh.homeIntro2: 1141年，希尔德加德在神的启示下开始撰写代表作《认识主道》（Scivias），历时十年完成。此后又完成了《生命的功业之书》（Liber Vitae Meritorum）和《神圣工程之书》（Liber Divinorum Operum）。这些著作包含关于宇宙秩序、教会命运和末世的深刻神视。
zh.homeIntro3: 2012年，教宗本笃十六世将希尔德加德封为教会圣师，使她成为天主教会仅有的四位女性教会圣师之一。她的预言以象征性的神视语言呈现，涉及教会腐败与净化、敌基督的降临、末世审判以及宇宙的终极秩序。本站整理了她著作中最具代表性的预言性神视。
zh.coreQuote: 「在这些日子里，正义将兴起，信仰之光将重新照亮世界。」
keyPointsList: 1098年 —— 生于莱茵河畔伯默斯海姆的贵族家庭
1141年 —— 受神谕开始撰写《认识主道》（Scivias）
1151年 —— 《认识主道》完成，获教宗尤金三世认可
1163年 —— 开始撰写《神圣工程之书》（Liber Divinorum Operum）
1179年 —— 9月17日逝世于宾根附近的吕佩尔茨贝格修道院
2012年 —— 教宗本笃十六世将其封为教会圣师
sourceList: 预言内容主要出自希尔德加德的三部神视著作：《认识主道》(Scivias)、《生命的功业之书》(Liber Vitae Meritorum)、《神圣工程之书》(Liber Divinorum Operum)（Wikipedia）
拉丁原文参考 Patrologia Latina 第197卷（Documenta Catholica Omnia）
验证评价基于历史事件记录和教会史研究
编者观点不代表学术定论
站点图标采用火焰符号——希尔德加德的神视常被描绘为「天降神火」，火焰是其神视画作中最具辨识度的视觉元素

en.homeIntro1: Hildegard of Bingen (1098 – September 17, 1179) was a German Benedictine abbess, mystic, composer, naturalist, and physician. From early childhood she experienced visions, which she described as seeing the 'Living Light' (Lux vivens), and recorded these experiences in several major works.
en.homeIntro2: In 1141, Hildegard received a divine command to begin writing her masterwork Scivias ('Know the Ways'), which took ten years to complete. She later wrote Liber Vitae Meritorum ('Book of Life's Merits') and Liber Divinorum Operum ('Book of Divine Works'). These works contain profound visions concerning cosmic order, the fate of the Church, and the end of days.
en.homeIntro3: In 2012, Pope Benedict XVI named Hildegard a Doctor of the Church, making her one of only four women to hold this title in Catholic history. Her prophecies are presented in symbolic visionary language, addressing Church corruption and purification, the coming of the Antichrist, the Last Judgment, and the ultimate order of the cosmos. This site compiles her most representative prophetic visions.
en.coreQuote: "In those days, justice will rise up, and the light of faith will shine upon the world anew."
keyPointsList: 1098 —— Born into a noble family near the Rhine in Bermersheim
1141 —— Received divine command to begin writing Scivias
1151 —— Scivias completed, approved by Pope Eugene III
1163 —— Began writing Liber Divinorum Operum (Book of Divine Works)
1179 —— Died on September 17 at Rupertsberg monastery near Bingen
2012 —— Named Doctor of the Church by Pope Benedict XVI
sourceList: Prophecies drawn from Hildegard's three major visionary works: Scivias, Liber Vitae Meritorum, and Liber Divinorum Operum (Wikipedia)
Latin texts referenced from Patrologia Latina vol. 197 (Documenta Catholica Omnia)
Verification based on historical records and Church history scholarship
Editorial opinions do not represent academic consensus
Site icon: flame symbol — Hildegard's visions were often depicted as divine fire descending from heaven, making the flame the most recognizable visual element in her illuminated manuscripts
